35	-- :ref:`sine_generator.sine_generator_pkg`.
36	--
37	-- The phase increment value can be calculated as
38	--
39	-- .. math::
40	--
41	--   \text{phase_increment} = \text{int} \left(
42	--     \frac{\text{sine_frequency_hz}}{\text{clk_frequency_hz}} \times 2^\text{phase_width}
43	--    \right).
44	--
45	-- Where ``sine_frequency_hz`` is the target sinus output frequency, and ``clk_frequency_hz`` is
46	-- the frequency of the system clock that is clocking this module.
47	-- In VHDL you are recommended to utilize the ``get_phase_increment`` function in
48	-- :ref:`sine_generator.sine_generator_pkg`.
49	--
50	-- Note that the Nyquist condition must be honored, meaning that the sine frequency must be less
51	-- than half the clock frequency.
52	--
53	--
54	-- .. _sine_generator_dithering:
55	--
56	-- Phase dithering
57	-- _______________
58	--
59	-- Phase dithering adds a pseudo-random offset to the phase that is sent
60	-- to :ref:`sine_generator.sine_calculator`.
61	-- The phase offset is uniformly distributed over the entire fractional phase width, meaning
62	-- between 0 and almost 1 LSB of the memory address.
63	-- The phase offset is added on top of the phase accumulator, and sometimes the addition will result
64	-- in +1 LSB in the address, sometimes it will not.
65	--
66	-- .. figure:: dithering_zoom.png
67	--
68	--   Zoom in of a low-frequency sine wave, without and with dithering.
69	--
70	-- This phase offset spreads out the spectrum distortion caused by phase truncation when
71	-- reading from memory.
72	-- The result is a lower peak distortion, i.e. a higher SFDR.
73	-- This comes, of course, at the cost of an increased overall level of noise, i.e. a lower SNDR.
74	-- Whether this tradeoff is worth it depends on the use case, and the choice is left to the user.
75	--
76	-- See :ref:`sine_phase_dithering` for a system-level perspective and some performance graphs.
77	--
78	--
79	-- Pseudo-random algorithm
80	-- ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
81	--
82	-- Dithering is implemented using a maximum-length linear feedback shift register (LFSR)
83	-- from the :ref:`module_lfsr`.
84	-- This gives a sequence of repeating state outputs that is not correlated with the phase and
85	-- appears pseudo-random.
86	--
87	-- The LFSR length is at least equal to the fractional width of the phase increment.
88	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
89	
90	
91	library ieee;
92	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
93	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
94	
95	library lfsr;
96	
97	library math;
98	use math.math_pkg.all;
99	
100	use work.sine_generator_pkg.all;
101	
102	
103	entity sine_generator is
104	  generic (
105	    -- The data width of sinusoid samples in ROM.
106	    -- Affects the SNDR/SFDR of the result.
107	    memory_data_width : positive;
108	    -- The number of bits in the address of sine samples in ROM.
109	    -- ROM will hold '2 ** memory_address_width' samples.
110	    -- Affects the frequency resolution of the module, as well as the SNDR/SFDR when
111	    -- in fractional phase mode, see module documentation.
112	    memory_address_width : positive;
113	    -- Set a non-zero value to enable fractional phase mode.
114	    -- Gives a better frequency resolution at the cost of worse performance due to phase truncation.
115	    phase_fractional_width : natural := 0;
116	    -- Enable the sine output ('result_sine' port).
117	    enable_sine : boolean := true;
118	    -- Enable the cosine output ('result_cosine' port).
119	    enable_cosine : boolean := false;
120	    -- When in fractional phase mode, improve SFDR (but worsen SNDR) by spreading out the phase
121	    -- error noise.
122	    enable_phase_dithering : boolean := false;
123	    -- When in fractional phase mode, improve SNDR and SFDR by using a first-order
124	    -- Taylor expansion of sinus samples.
125	    enable_first_order_taylor : boolean := false;
126	    -- Can optionally set the initial value of the phase counter.
127	    -- Useful when you want multiple sine generators to be in different phase.
128	    -- Note that the actual phase is slightly offset from the value specified here, see
129	    -- module documentation.
130	    initial_phase : u_unsigned(
131	      get_phase_width(
132	        memory_address_width=>memory_address_width, phase_fractional_width=>phase_fractional_width
133	      ) - 1
134	      downto
135	      0
136	    ) := (others => '0')
137	  );
138	  port(
139	    clk : in std_ulogic;
140	    --# {{}}
141	    input_valid : in std_ulogic;
142	    input_phase_increment : in u_unsigned(initial_phase'range);
143	    --# {{}}
144	    result_valid : out std_ulogic := '0';
145	    result_sine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
146	    result_cosine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0')
147	  );
148	end entity;
149	
150	architecture a of sine_generator is
151	
152	  signal phase_accumulator_valid, phase_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
153	  signal phase_accumulator, phase : u_unsigned(input_phase_increment'range) := initial_phase;
154	
155	begin
156	
157	  assert not (enable_phase_dithering and enable_first_order_taylor)
158	    report "Dithering will ruin the performance of Taylor expansion. Do not enable both."
159	    severity failure;
160	
161	
162	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
163	  assert_widths_block : block
164	    function get_result_enob return positive is
165	    begin
166	      -- Integer phase mode.
167	      if phase_fractional_width = 0 then
168	        return memory_data_width + 1;
169	      end if;
170	
171	      -- Fractional phase mode, with dithering.
172	      if enable_phase_dithering then
173	        return memory_address_width + 4;
174	      end if;
175	
176	      -- Fractional phase mode, with Taylor.
177	      if enable_first_order_taylor then
178	        return 2 * (memory_address_width + 1);
179	      end if;
180	
181	      -- Base fractional phase mode.
182	      return memory_address_width + 1;
183	    end function;
184	    constant result_enob : positive := get_result_enob;
185	  begin
186	
187	    -- Assert that we are not unnecessarily limiting the performance of the module.
188	    -- See module documentation for a background on the limits.
189	    assert memory_data_width >= result_enob - 1
190	      report (
191	        "Memory data width is limiting performance. Need at least " &
192	        integer'image(result_enob - 1) & " bits"
193	      )
194	      severity failure;
195	
196	  end block;
197	
198	
199	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
200	  phase_counter : process
201	    variable phase_increment_to_add : u_unsigned(phase_accumulator'length - 1 - 1 downto 0) := (
202	      others => '0'
203	    );
204	  begin
205	    wait until rising_edge(clk);
206	
207	    phase_accumulator_valid <= input_valid;
208	
209	    if input_valid then
210	      -- When the phase increment is as wide as the phase accumulator, the top bit
211	      -- may not be used since that would break the Nyquist criterion.
212	      -- Hence we can remove the top bit from the addition, saving one LUT.
213	      phase_increment_to_add := input_phase_increment(phase_increment_to_add'range);
214	      assert input_phase_increment(input_phase_increment'high) /= '1'
215	        report "Too high frequency";
216	
217	      -- Overflow is expected and desired.
218	      -- Fixed-point numbers are periodic just like the sine function.
219	      phase_accumulator <= phase_accumulator + phase_increment_to_add;
220	    end if;
221	  end process;
222	
223	
224	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
225	  phase_dithering_gen : if enable_phase_dithering generate
226	    signal phase_dithering : u_unsigned(phase_fractional_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
227	    signal phase_dithering_slv : std_ulogic_vector(phase_dithering'range) := (others => '0');
228	  begin
229	
230	    assert phase_fractional_width > 0
231	      report "Phase dithering requires at least 1 fractional phase bit"
232	      severity failure;
233	
234	    -- Some test cases with 4 bits would sometimes be 0.5 dB too low SFDR.
235	    assert memory_address_width >= 6
236	      report "Phase dithering does not work well if the memory is too small"
237	      severity failure;
238	
239	
240	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	    lfsr_fibonacci_multi_inst : entity lfsr.lfsr_fibonacci_multi
242	      generic map (
243	        -- Set a lower limit so we get at least some guaranteed level of randomization.
244	        -- Quite arbitrarily chosen.
245	        -- Works well in all test cases though, and is not too big in terms of resources.
246	        minimum_lfsr_length => 10,
247	        output_width => phase_dithering_slv'length
248	      )
249	      port map (
250	        clk => clk,
251	        --
252	        output => phase_dithering_slv
253	      );
254	
255	    phase_dithering <= u_unsigned(phase_dithering_slv);
256	
257	
258	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	    assign_phase : process
260	    begin
261	      wait until rising_edge(clk);
262	
263	      phase_valid <= phase_accumulator_valid;
264	
265	      -- Note that this might overflow, but this is desired behavior.
266	      -- If integer phase accumulator is on the very last address, we will jump back and forth
267	      -- between the last and the first address, depending on the random dither.
268	      phase <= phase_accumulator + phase_dithering;
269	    end process;
270	
271	
272	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
273	  else generate
274	
275	    phase_valid <= phase_accumulator_valid;
276	    phase <= phase_accumulator;
277	
278	  end generate;
279	
280	
281	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
282	  sine_calculator_inst : entity work.sine_calculator
283	    generic map (
284	      memory_data_width => memory_data_width,
285	      memory_address_width => memory_address_width,
286	      phase_fractional_width => phase_fractional_width,
287	      enable_sine => enable_sine,
288	      enable_cosine => enable_cosine,
289	      enable_first_order_taylor => enable_first_order_taylor
290	    )
291	    port map (
292	      clk => clk,
293	      --
294	      input_valid => phase_valid,
295	      input_phase => phase,
296	      --
297	      result_valid => result_valid,
298	      result_sine => result_sine,
299	      result_cosine => result_cosine
300	    );
301	
302	end architecture;
303

file.content:
-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
--
-- This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
-- peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
-- https://hdl-modules.com
-- https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- This sinus generator top level accumulates the incoming ``phase_increment`` to form a
-- phase value.
-- The :ref:`sine_generator.sine_calculator` is instantiated to calculate sinusoid values
-- based on this phase.
--
-- Set the ``enable_sine`` and ``enable_cosine`` generic parameters to enable sine and/or
-- cosine output.
--
-- If fractional phase is enabled, the fractional part of the phase will be truncated in
-- :ref:`sine_generator.sine_calculator` when forming the lookup address.
-- Phase dithering can be enabled in this case to improve the SFDR.
--
--
-- .. _sine_calculate_increment:
--
-- About phase increment
-- _____________________
--
-- The frequency of the output signal is determined by the ``phase_increment`` input port value.
-- The width of this port is equal to
--
-- .. math::
--
--   \text{phase_width} = \text{memory_address_width} + 2 + \text{phase_fractional_width}
--
-- In VHDL you are recommended to utilize the ``get_phase_width`` function in
-- :ref:`sine_generator.sine_generator_pkg`.
--
-- The phase increment value can be calculated as
--
-- .. math::
--
--   \text{phase_increment} = \text{int} \left(
--     \frac{\text{sine_frequency_hz}}{\text{clk_frequency_hz}} \times 2^\text{phase_width}
--    \right).
--
-- Where ``sine_frequency_hz`` is the target sinus output frequency, and ``clk_frequency_hz`` is
-- the frequency of the system clock that is clocking this module.
-- In VHDL you are recommended to utilize the ``get_phase_increment`` function in
-- :ref:`sine_generator.sine_generator_pkg`.
--
-- Note that the Nyquist condition must be honored, meaning that the sine frequency must be less
-- than half the clock frequency.
--
--
-- .. _sine_generator_dithering:
--
-- Phase dithering
-- _______________
--
-- Phase dithering adds a pseudo-random offset to the phase that is sent
-- to :ref:`sine_generator.sine_calculator`.
-- The phase offset is uniformly distributed over the entire fractional phase width, meaning
-- between 0 and almost 1 LSB of the memory address.
-- The phase offset is added on top of the phase accumulator, and sometimes the addition will result
-- in +1 LSB in the address, sometimes it will not.
--
-- .. figure:: dithering_zoom.png
--
--   Zoom in of a low-frequency sine wave, without and with dithering.
--
-- This phase offset spreads out the spectrum distortion caused by phase truncation when
-- reading from memory.
-- The result is a lower peak distortion, i.e. a higher SFDR.
-- This comes, of course, at the cost of an increased overall level of noise, i.e. a lower SNDR.
-- Whether this tradeoff is worth it depends on the use case, and the choice is left to the user.
--
-- See :ref:`sine_phase_dithering` for a system-level perspective and some performance graphs.
--
--
-- Pseudo-random algorithm
-- ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
--
-- Dithering is implemented using a maximum-length linear feedback shift register (LFSR)
-- from the :ref:`module_lfsr`.
-- This gives a sequence of repeating state outputs that is not correlated with the phase and
-- appears pseudo-random.
--
-- The LFSR length is at least equal to the fractional width of the phase increment.
-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------


library ieee;
use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
use ieee.numeric_std.all;

library lfsr;

library math;
use math.math_pkg.all;

use work.sine_generator_pkg.all;


entity sine_generator is
  generic (
    -- The data width of sinusoid samples in ROM.
    -- Affects the SNDR/SFDR of the result.
    memory_data_width : positive;
    -- The number of bits in the address of sine samples in ROM.
    -- ROM will hold '2 ** memory_address_width' samples.
    -- Affects the frequency resolution of the module, as well as the SNDR/SFDR when
    -- in fractional phase mode, see module documentation.
    memory_address_width : positive;
    -- Set a non-zero value to enable fractional phase mode.
    -- Gives a better frequency resolution at the cost of worse performance due to phase truncation.
    phase_fractional_width : natural := 0;
    -- Enable the sine output ('result_sine' port).
    enable_sine : boolean := true;
    -- Enable the cosine output ('result_cosine' port).
    enable_cosine : boolean := false;
    -- When in fractional phase mode, improve SFDR (but worsen SNDR) by spreading out the phase
    -- error noise.
    enable_phase_dithering : boolean := false;
    -- When in fractional phase mode, improve SNDR and SFDR by using a first-order
    -- Taylor expansion of sinus samples.
    enable_first_order_taylor : boolean := false;
    -- Can optionally set the initial value of the phase counter.
    -- Useful when you want multiple sine generators to be in different phase.
    -- Note that the actual phase is slightly offset from the value specified here, see
    -- module documentation.
    initial_phase : u_unsigned(
      get_phase_width(
        memory_address_width=>memory_address_width, phase_fractional_width=>phase_fractional_width
      ) - 1
      downto
      0
    ) := (others => '0')
  );
  port(
    clk : in std_ulogic;
    --# {{}}
    input_valid : in std_ulogic;
    input_phase_increment : in u_unsigned(initial_phase'range);
    --# {{}}
    result_valid : out std_ulogic := '0';
    result_sine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
    result_cosine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0')
  );
end entity;

architecture a of sine_generator is

  signal phase_accumulator_valid, phase_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
  signal phase_accumulator, phase : u_unsigned(input_phase_increment'range) := initial_phase;

begin

  assert not (enable_phase_dithering and enable_first_order_taylor)
    report "Dithering will ruin the performance of Taylor expansion. Do not enable both."
    severity failure;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  assert_widths_block : block
    function get_result_enob return positive is
    begin
      -- Integer phase mode.
      if phase_fractional_width = 0 then
        return memory_data_width + 1;
      end if;

      -- Fractional phase mode, with dithering.
      if enable_phase_dithering then
        return memory_address_width + 4;
      end if;

      -- Fractional phase mode, with Taylor.
      if enable_first_order_taylor then
        return 2 * (memory_address_width + 1);
      end if;

      -- Base fractional phase mode.
      return memory_address_width + 1;
    end function;
    constant result_enob : positive := get_result_enob;
  begin

    -- Assert that we are not unnecessarily limiting the performance of the module.
    -- See module documentation for a background on the limits.
    assert memory_data_width >= result_enob - 1
      report (
        "Memory data width is limiting performance. Need at least " &
        integer'image(result_enob - 1) & " bits"
      )
      severity failure;

  end block;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  phase_counter : process
    variable phase_increment_to_add : u_unsigned(phase_accumulator'length - 1 - 1 downto 0) := (
      others => '0'
    );
  begin
    wait until rising_edge(clk);

    phase_accumulator_valid <= input_valid;

    if input_valid then
      -- When the phase increment is as wide as the phase accumulator, the top bit
      -- may not be used since that would break the Nyquist criterion.
      -- Hence we can remove the top bit from the addition, saving one LUT.
      phase_increment_to_add := input_phase_increment(phase_increment_to_add'range);
      assert input_phase_increment(input_phase_increment'high) /= '1'
        report "Too high frequency";

      -- Overflow is expected and desired.
      -- Fixed-point numbers are periodic just like the sine function.
      phase_accumulator <= phase_accumulator + phase_increment_to_add;
    end if;
  end process;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  phase_dithering_gen : if enable_phase_dithering generate
    signal phase_dithering : u_unsigned(phase_fractional_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
    signal phase_dithering_slv : std_ulogic_vector(phase_dithering'range) := (others => '0');
  begin

    assert phase_fractional_width > 0
      report "Phase dithering requires at least 1 fractional phase bit"
      severity failure;

    -- Some test cases with 4 bits would sometimes be 0.5 dB too low SFDR.
    assert memory_address_width >= 6
      report "Phase dithering does not work well if the memory is too small"
      severity failure;


    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    lfsr_fibonacci_multi_inst : entity lfsr.lfsr_fibonacci_multi
      generic map (
        -- Set a lower limit so we get at least some guaranteed level of randomization.
        -- Quite arbitrarily chosen.
        -- Works well in all test cases though, and is not too big in terms of resources.
        minimum_lfsr_length => 10,
        output_width => phase_dithering_slv'length
      )
      port map (
        clk => clk,
        --
        output => phase_dithering_slv
      );

    phase_dithering <= u_unsigned(phase_dithering_slv);


    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    assign_phase : process
    begin
      wait until rising_edge(clk);

      phase_valid <= phase_accumulator_valid;

      -- Note that this might overflow, but this is desired behavior.
      -- If integer phase accumulator is on the very last address, we will jump back and forth
      -- between the last and the first address, depending on the random dither.
      phase <= phase_accumulator + phase_dithering;
    end process;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  else generate

    phase_valid <= phase_accumulator_valid;
    phase <= phase_accumulator;

  end generate;


  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  sine_calculator_inst : entity work.sine_calculator
    generic map (
      memory_data_width => memory_data_width,
      memory_address_width => memory_address_width,
      phase_fractional_width => phase_fractional_width,
      enable_sine => enable_sine,
      enable_cosine => enable_cosine,
      enable_first_order_taylor => enable_first_order_taylor
    )
    port map (
      clk => clk,
      --
      input_valid => phase_valid,
      input_phase => phase,
      --
      result_valid => result_valid,
      result_sine => result_sine,
      result_cosine => result_cosine
    );

end architecture;

content.content:
1	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	-- Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
3	--
4	-- This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
5	-- peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
6	-- https://hdl-modules.com
7	-- https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
8	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9	-- Package with constants/types/functions for the sine generator ecosystem.
10	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
11	
12	library ieee;
13	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
14	use ieee.math_real.all;
15	
16	
17	package sine_generator_pkg is
18	
19	  -- Get the width of the phase accumulator and the phase increment port.
20	  -- Set arguments to the same values as the generic values to your entity instantiation.
21	  function get_phase_width(
22	    memory_address_width : positive; phase_fractional_width : natural
23	  ) return positive;
24	
25	  -- Get the phase increment needed to synthesize a sine wave of the given frequency.
26	  -- Note that this function will truncate the phase increment to the nearest
27	  -- 'phase_accumulator_width' integer.
28	  -- It is up to the user to analyze the frequency resolution requirements of the application (see
29	  -- module documentation).
30	  function get_phase_increment(
31	    -- Switching frequency of the clock signal to the module.
32	    clk_frequency_hz : positive;
33	    -- Target frequency of the generated sine wave.
34	    sine_frequency_hz : positive;
35	    -- Number of bits in the phase counter, provided by the 'get_phase_width' function give your
36	    -- generic parameterization of the module.
37	    phase_width : positive
38	  ) return u_unsigned;
39	
40	end package;
41	
42	package body sine_generator_pkg is
43	
44	  function get_phase_width(
45	    memory_address_width : positive; phase_fractional_width : natural
46	  ) return positive is
47	  begin
48	    -- +2 for the quadrant indicator, given that only one quadrant is stored in memory.
49	    return memory_address_width + 2 + phase_fractional_width;
50	  end function;
51	
52	  function get_phase_increment(
53	    clk_frequency_hz : positive;
54	    sine_frequency_hz : positive;
55	    phase_width : positive
56	  ) return u_unsigned is
57	    constant nyquist_frequency_hz : real := real(clk_frequency_hz) / 2.0;
58	    constant max_increment : real := 2.0 ** real(phase_width);
59	    constant ratio : real := real(sine_frequency_hz) / real(clk_frequency_hz);
60	    constant target_increment : real := ratio * max_increment;
61	
62	    constant result : u_unsigned(phase_width - 1 downto 0) := to_unsigned(
63	      integer(round(target_increment)), phase_width
64	    );
65	  begin
66	    assert real(sine_frequency_hz) < nyquist_frequency_hz
67	      report "Cannot synthesize this sine wave"
68	      severity failure;
69	
70	    assert result > 0 report "Phase increment is zero" severity failure;
71	
72	    return result;
73	  end function;
74	
75	end package body;
76

1	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	-- Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
3	--
4	-- This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
5	-- peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
6	-- https://hdl-modules.com
7	-- https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
8	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9	-- Calculates the sinus value corresponding to the provided phase value.
10	-- Instantiates a sine lookup table (:ref:`sine_generator.sine_lookup`) where the integer part
11	-- of the phase will be used as address.
12	--
13	-- If fractional phase is enabled, the fractional part of the phase will be truncated when
14	-- forming the lookup address.
15	-- First-order Taylor expansion can be enabled to improve the accuracy using the truncated phase.
16	--
17	--
18	-- .. _sine_taylor_expansion:
19	--
20	-- Taylor expansion
21	-- ________________
22	--
23	-- The Taylor expansion of a function is given by
24	--
25	-- .. math::
26	--
27	--   f(x) = f(a) + f'(a) \times (x - a) + \frac{f''(a)}{2!} \times (x - a)^2
28	--     + \frac{f'''(a)}{3!} \times (x - a)^3 + ...
29	--
30	-- See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taylor_series.
31	-- The accuracy is better if :math:`a` is close to :math:`x`, or if many terms are used.
32	-- Substituting
33	--
34	-- .. math::
35	--
36	--   e \equiv x - a,
37	--
38	-- and realizing the following properties of the derivative of the sine function
39	--
40	-- .. math::
41	--
42	--   f(x) & \equiv A \sin(B x + C) \\
43	--   \Rightarrow f'(x) & = A B \cos(B x + C) \\
44	--   \Rightarrow f''(x) & = -A B^2 \sin(B x + C) = - B^2 f(x) \\
45	--   \Rightarrow f'''(x) & = -A B^3 \cos(B x + C) = - B^2 f'(x) \\
46	--   \Rightarrow f''''(x) & = A B^4 \sin(B x + C) = B^4 f(x)
47	--
48	-- we get
49	--
50	-- .. math::
51	--
52	--   A \sin(B x + C) = & \ A \sin(B a + C) \times
53	--   \left( 1 - \frac{(Be)^2}{2!} + \frac{(Be)^4}{4!}  - \frac{(Be)^6}{6!} + \ldots \right) \\
54	--   & + A \cos(B a + C) \times
55	--   \left( Be - \frac{(Be)^3}{3!} + \frac{(Be)^5}{5!} - \frac{(Be)^7}{7!} + \ldots \right).
56	--
57	--
58	-- Taylor expansion implementation
59	-- _______________________________
60	--
61	-- This entity corrects the sine lookup value using first-order Taylor expansion, meaning
62	--
63	-- .. math::
64	--
65	--  A \sin(B x + C) \approx A \sin(B a + C) + A \cos(B a + C) \times B e.
66	--
67	-- In this representation, :math:`x` is the full phase value, including fractional bits.
68	-- The :math:`a` is the integer part of the phase value that forms the
69	-- memory address, and :math:`e` is the fractional part of the phase that gets truncated.
70	-- The :math:`A \sin(B x + C)` and :math:`A \cos(B x + C)` values are given by
71	-- :ref:`sine_generator.sine_lookup`.
72	-- The :math:`B` value is the phase increment of the lookup table:
73	--
74	-- .. math::
75	--
76	--   B \equiv \frac{\pi / 2}{2^\text{memory_address_width}}.
77	--
78	-- The calculation is partitioned like this, using DSP48 blocks:
79	--
80	-- .. digraph:: my_graph
81	--
82	--   graph [dpi=300];
83	--   rankdir="LR";
84	--
85	--   phase_error [shape="none" label="phase error"];
86	--   pi [shape="none" label="π/2"];
87	--
88	--   {
89	--     rank=same;
90	--     phase_error;
91	--     pi;
92	--   }
93	--
94	--   first_multiplication [shape="box" label="x"];
95	--
96	--   phase_error -> first_multiplication [label="<=25"];
97	--   pi -> first_multiplication [label="<=18"];
98	--
99	--   lookup_cosine [shape="none" label="lookup cosine"];
100	--
101	--   {
102	--     first_multiplication=same;
103	--     lookup_cosine;
104	--     pi;
105	--   }
106	--
107	--   second_multiplication [shape="box" label="x"];
108	--
109	--   first_multiplication -> second_multiplication [label="<=25"];
110	--   lookup_cosine -> second_multiplication [label="<=18"];
111	--
112	--   lookup_sine [shape="none" label="lookup sine & 0"];
113	--
114	--   {
115	--     first_multiplication=same;
116	--     second_multiplication;
117	--     lookup_sine;
118	--   }
119	--
120	--   addition [shape="box" label="+"];
121	--
122	--   second_multiplication -> addition [label="<=47"];
123	--   lookup_sine -> addition [label="<=47"];
124	--
125	--   saturation [shape="box" label="saturation"];
126	--
127	--   addition -> saturation [label="<=48"];
128	--
129	--   result [shape="none" label="result"];
130	--
131	--   saturation -> result;
132	--
133	-- The :math:`\pi / 2` is handled as a fixed-point value with a number of fractional bits
134	-- determined to give sufficient performance.
135	-- Multiplying with the phase error, which is a fractional value, and then the cosine value
136	-- gives a value that has a very high number of fractional bits.
137	-- In order for the summation with the sine value to be correct, the sine value must be
138	-- shifted up until the binal points align.
139	--
140	-- When the operands are small, the last multiplication and the addition can fit in the same DSP48.
141	-- This is not the case in general though.
142	--
143	-- .. note::
144	--
145	--   An alternative approach would be to store the error term in a ROM and use the phase error
146	--   as lookup address.
147	--   This would save DSP blocks but cost BRAM.
148	--   We can support that in the future with a generic switch if there is ever a need.
149	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
150	
151	library ieee;
152	use ieee.math_real.all;
153	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
154	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
155	
156	library common;
157	use common.attribute_pkg.all;
158	use common.types_pkg.all;
159	
160	library math;
161	use math.math_pkg.all;
162	
163	use work.sine_generator_pkg.all;
164	
165	
166	entity sine_calculator is
167	  generic (
168	    -- The data width of sinusoid samples in ROM.
169	    -- Affects the SNDR/SFDR of the result.
170	    memory_data_width : positive;
171	    -- The number of bits in the address of sine samples in ROM.
172	    -- ROM will hold '2 ** memory_address_width' samples.
173	    -- Affects the frequency resolution of the module, as well as the SNDR/SFDR when
174	    -- in fractional phase mode, see module documentation.
175	    memory_address_width : positive;
176	    -- Enable the sine output ('result_sine' port).
177	    enable_sine : boolean := true;
178	    -- Enable the cosine output ('result_cosine' port).
179	    enable_cosine : boolean := false;
180	    -- Set a non-zero value to enable fractional phase mode.
181	    -- Gives a better frequency resolution at the cost of worse performance due to phase truncation.
182	    phase_fractional_width : natural;
183	    -- When in fractional phase mode, improve SFDR (but worsen SNDR) by spreading out the phase
184	    -- error noise.
185	    enable_first_order_taylor : boolean
186	  );
187	  port(
188	    clk : in std_ulogic;
189	    --# {{}}
190	    input_valid : in std_ulogic;
191	    input_phase : in u_unsigned(
192	      get_phase_width(
193	        memory_address_width=>memory_address_width, phase_fractional_width=>phase_fractional_width
194	      ) - 1
195	      downto
196	      0
197	    );
198	    --# {{}}
199	    result_valid : out std_ulogic := '0';
200	    result_sine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
201	    result_cosine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0')
202	  );
203	end entity;
204	
205	architecture a of sine_calculator is
206	
207	  signal input_phase_integer : u_unsigned(memory_address_width + 2 - 1 downto 0) := (
208	    others => '0'
209	  );
210	  signal input_phase_fractional : u_unsigned(
211	    input_phase'high - input_phase_integer'length downto 0
212	  ) := (others => '0');
213	
214	  signal lookup_valid : std_ulogic := '0';
215	  signal lookup_sine, lookup_cosine : u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (
216	    others => '0'
217	  );
218	
219	begin
220	
221	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
222	  sine_lookup_inst : entity work.sine_lookup
223	    generic map (
224	      memory_data_width => memory_data_width,
225	      memory_address_width => memory_address_width,
226	      enable_sine => enable_sine or enable_first_order_taylor,
227	      enable_cosine => enable_cosine or enable_first_order_taylor
228	    )
229	    port map (
230	      clk => clk,
231	      --
232	      input_valid => input_valid,
233	      input_phase => input_phase_integer,
234	      --
235	      result_valid => lookup_valid,
236	      result_sine => lookup_sine,
237	      result_cosine => lookup_cosine
238	    );
239	
240	  input_phase_integer <= input_phase(
241	    input_phase'high downto input_phase'length - input_phase_integer'length
242	  );
243	
244	  input_phase_fractional <= input_phase(input_phase_fractional'range);
245	
246	
247	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
248	  taylor_expansion_gen : if enable_first_order_taylor generate
249	    constant core_latency : positive := work.taylor_expansion_core'latency;
250	    signal valid_pipe : std_ulogic_vector(1 to core_latency) := (others => '0');
251	
252	    -- Referring to the documentation, we want to scale the fractional fixed-point
253	    -- 'phase_error' with the factor 'B'.
254	    -- Which is pi/2 / 2**memory_address_width.
255	    -- The '2**address_width' part we will realize as a shift of the multiplication result.
256	    -- But the 'pi/2' we have to make as a multiplication.
257	    -- The number of fractional bits below has been found by trial-and-error to not be limiting
258	    -- the performance of the system in any of the performance modes.
259	    -- This width also makes it map nicely to a DSP48E1 (25x18 + 48 = 48)
260	    -- or DSP48E2 (27x18 + 48 = 48)
261	    constant scale_factor_fractional_width : positive := 16;
262	    constant scale_factor_real : real := MATH_PI_OVER_2 * 2.0 ** scale_factor_fractional_width;
263	    constant scale_factor_int : positive := integer(round(scale_factor_real));
264	    constant scale_factor_width : positive := num_bits_needed(scale_factor_int);
265	    constant scale_factor : u_unsigned(scale_factor_width - 1 downto 0) := to_unsigned(
266	      scale_factor_int, scale_factor_width
267	    );
268	
269	    -- In order to map 'error_factor = phase_error * scale_factor' to a DSP48 we might, in a very
270	    -- unlikely scenario, have to trim the 'phase_error'.
271	    -- In case of a huge phase accumulator, the error might be wider than 24 (meaning a signed 25).
272	    -- We have assertions below that this trimming does not limit the performance of the output.
273	    constant phase_error24_width : positive := minimum(input_phase_fractional'length, 24);
274	    -- The number of bits we trimmed to get to the desired width.
275	    constant phase_error24_trim : natural := input_phase_fractional'length - phase_error24_width;
276	
277	    -- +1 for unsigned -> signed.
278	    constant error_factor_width : positive := phase_error24_width + scale_factor'length + 1;
279	    -- We might have to trim to make the 'derivative_term = error_factor * derivative'
280	    -- multiplication fit in a DSP48.
281	    constant error_factor25_width : positive := minimum(error_factor_width, 25);
282	    -- Number of bits we trimmed to get to the desired width.
283	    constant error_factor25_trim : natural := error_factor_width - error_factor25_width;
284	
285	    -- The 'value' term needs to be shifted up this many steps for the binal point to align with
286	    -- the 'derivative' term.
287	    -- In this nomenclature we do not take into account any fraction width or fixed-point
288	    -- representation of the lookup sinusoid values, since that is the same for both terms.
289	    -- This number is only the difference between the two terms.
290	    constant error_factor25_fractional_width : positive := (
291	      phase_error24_width
292	      + scale_factor_fractional_width
293	      + memory_address_width
294	      - error_factor25_trim
295	    );
296	
297	    signal first_stage_error_factor25 : u_signed(error_factor25_width - 1 downto 0) := (
298	      others => '0'
299	    );
300	
301	    attribute use_dsp of first_stage_error_factor25 : signal is "yes";
302	  begin
303	
304	    assert enable_sine or enable_cosine
305	      report "Enable at least one output signal"
306	      severity failure;
307	
308	    assert memory_address_width <= 15
309	      report "Performance has not been verified with this accuracy"
310	      severity failure;
311	
312	    assert input_phase_fractional'length > 0
313	      report "Taylor expansion requires at least 1 fractional phase bit"
314	      severity failure;
315	
316	
317	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
318	    print : process
319	    begin
320	      report "======================================================================";
321	      report "scale_factor'length = " & integer'image(scale_factor'length);
322	      report "input_phase_fractional'length = " & integer'image(input_phase_fractional'length);
323	
324	      report "======================================================================";
325	      report "phase_error24_width = " & integer'image(phase_error24_width);
326	      report "phase_error24_trim = " & integer'image(phase_error24_trim);
327	
328	      report "======================================================================";
329	      report "error_factor_width = " & integer'image(error_factor_width);
330	      report "error_factor25_width = " & integer'image(error_factor25_width);
331	      report "error_factor25_trim = " & integer'image(error_factor25_trim);
332	      report "error_factor25_fractional_width = " & integer'image(error_factor25_fractional_width);
333	
334	      wait;
335	    end process;
336	
337	
338	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
339	    pipeline_valid : process
340	    begin
341	      wait until rising_edge(clk);
342	
343	      valid_pipe <= lookup_valid & valid_pipe(valid_pipe'left to valid_pipe'right - 1);
344	    end process;
345	
346	    result_valid <= valid_pipe(valid_pipe'right);
347	
348	
349	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
350	    first_stage : block
351	      signal phase_error24, input_phase_fractional24 : u_unsigned(
352	        phase_error24_width - 1 downto 0
353	      ) := (others => '0');
354	
355	      -- We want to access the error value one cycle before we access the lookup result,
356	      -- hence the range of the pipe.
357	      constant lookup_latency : positive := work.sine_lookup'latency;
358	      signal phase_error24_pipe : unsigned_vec_t(1 to lookup_latency - 1)(phase_error24'range) := (
359	        others => (others => '0')
360	      );
361	
362	      -- Full size.
363	      signal error_factor_unsigned : u_unsigned(error_factor_width - 1 - 1 downto 0) := (
364	        others => '0'
365	      );
366	      signal error_factor : u_signed(error_factor_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
367	
368	      attribute use_dsp of error_factor_unsigned : signal is "yes";
369	    begin
370	
371	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
372	      pipe : process
373	      begin
374	        wait until rising_edge(clk);
375	
376	        phase_error24_pipe <= (
377	          input_phase_fractional24
378	          & phase_error24_pipe(phase_error24_pipe'left to phase_error24_pipe'right - 1)
379	        );
380	      end process;
381	
382	      input_phase_fractional24 <= input_phase_fractional(
383	        input_phase_fractional'high downto phase_error24_trim
384	      );
385	
386	      phase_error24 <= phase_error24_pipe(phase_error24_pipe'right);
387	
388	
389	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
390	      assert scale_factor'length = 17
391	        report "Will not map nicely to DSP48. Got " & integer'image(scale_factor'length)
392	        severity failure;
393	
394	      assert phase_error24'length <= 24
395	        report "Will not map nicely to DSP48. Got " & integer'image(phase_error24'length)
396	        severity failure;
397	
398	
399	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
400	      calculate_first_stage : process
401	      begin
402	        wait until rising_edge(clk);
403	
404	        -- Calculate the error factor one cycle before the corresponding lookup result is available.
405	        -- An alternative approach would be to save this error factor in a ROM, and look it up
406	        -- using the phase error as address.
407	        -- This would save DSP but cost BRAM.
408	        error_factor_unsigned <= phase_error24 * scale_factor;
409	      end process;
410	
411	      error_factor <= '0' & u_signed(error_factor_unsigned);
412	      first_stage_error_factor25 <= error_factor(error_factor'high downto error_factor25_trim);
413	
414	    end block;
415	
416	
417	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
418	    sine_taylor_gen : if enable_sine generate
419	
420	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
421	      sine_taylor_expansion_core_inst : entity work.taylor_expansion_core
422	        generic map (
423	          sinusoid_width => lookup_sine'length,
424	          error_factor_width => first_stage_error_factor25'length,
425	          error_factor_fractional_width => error_factor25_fractional_width,
426	          result_width => result_sine'length,
427	          minus_derivative => false
428	        )
429	        port map (
430	          clk => clk,
431	          --
432	          input_value => lookup_sine,
433	          input_derivative => lookup_cosine,
434	          input_error_factor => first_stage_error_factor25,
435	          --
436	          result_value => result_sine
437	        );
438	
439	    end generate;
440	
441	
442	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
443	    cosine_taylor_gen : if enable_cosine generate
444	
445	      ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
446	      sine_taylor_expansion_core_inst : entity work.taylor_expansion_core
447	        generic map (
448	          sinusoid_width => lookup_sine'length,
449	          error_factor_width => first_stage_error_factor25'length,
450	          error_factor_fractional_width => error_factor25_fractional_width,
451	          result_width => result_cosine'length,
452	          minus_derivative => true
453	        )
454	        port map (
455	          clk => clk,
456	          --
457	          input_value => lookup_cosine,
458	          input_derivative => lookup_sine,
459	          input_error_factor => first_stage_error_factor25,
460	          --
461	          result_value => result_cosine
462	        );
463	
464	    end generate;
465	
466	  else generate
467	
468	    -- Simply assign output.
469	    result_valid <= lookup_valid;
470	
471	
472	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
473	    assign_sine : if enable_sine generate
474	      result_sine <= lookup_sine;
475	    end generate;
476	
477	
478	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
479	    assign_cosine : if enable_cosine generate
480	      result_cosine <= lookup_cosine;
481	    end generate;
482	
483	  end generate;
484	
485	end architecture;
486

content.content:
1	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	-- Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
3	--
4	-- This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
5	-- peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
6	-- https://hdl-modules.com
7	-- https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
8	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9	-- A lookup table for fixed-point sine and cosine values.
10	-- The ``input_phase`` is in range :math:`[0, 2 \pi[`, but the memory in this entity stores only
11	-- samples for :math:`[0, \pi / 2[`.
12	-- The phase is furthermore offset by plus half an LSB (see :ref:`sine_lookup_quadrant` below).
13	--
14	-- Each sample value in memory is ``memory_data_width`` bits wide.
15	--
16	-- Use the ``enable_*`` generics to specify which signals to calculate.
17	-- Enabling many is convenient when you want sinusoids that are perfectly :math:`\pi / 2`-offset
18	-- from each other.
19	-- Enabling further signals will not require any extra memory, but it will add logic.
20	-- Also, sine calculation (positive or negative) requires one memory read port, while cosine
21	-- calculation (positive or negative) requires another.
22	-- So enabling any sine along with any cosine will require two memory read ports.
23	--
24	--
25	-- .. _sine_lookup_quadrant:
26	--
27	-- Quadrant handling
28	-- _________________
29	--
30	-- Consider the unit circle, and the sine and cosine plots in the picture below.
31	--
32	-- .. figure:: quadrants.png
33	--
34	--   Overview of the four quadrants.
35	--
36	-- When implementing an angle-discrete sine lookup table, the first approach might be to use
37	-- the blue points in the plot above, starting at phase zero.
38	-- However, for the implementation to be efficient, we want to be able to calculate e.g. sine in
39	-- quadrant one as the sine in quadrant zero, but read out in reverse order.
40	-- This is desirable since "reverse order" when working with fixed-point numbers simply means
41	-- a bit-wise inverse of a "normal order" counter.
42	--
43	-- With this goal of efficient implementation in mind, we offset the phase so that the points are
44	-- mirrored around :math:`0`, :math:`\pi/2`, :math:`\pi` and :math:`3 \pi/2`.
45	-- The resulting symmetry can be clearly seen in the sine and cosine plots above.
46	-- For example, the sine points in quadrant one are clearly the same points as in quadrant zero,
47	-- but in reverse order.
48	-- Apart from this ocular hint, we can also show it using basic trigonometric identities:
49	--
50	-- .. math::
51	--
52	--   \text{Sine quadrant 0: } & \sin(\theta) = \sin(\theta)
53	--   \\
54	--   \text{Sine quadrant 1: } & \sin(\theta + \frac{\pi}{2}) = \sin(\frac{\pi}{2} - \theta)
55	--     = \sin( \bar{\theta} )
56	--   \\
57	--   \text{Sine quadrant 2: } & \sin(\theta + \pi) = - \sin(\theta)
58	--   \\
59	--   \text{Sine quadrant 3: } & \sin(\theta + \frac{3 \pi}{2}) = - \sin(\frac{\pi}{2} - \theta)
60	--     = - \sin( \bar{\theta} )
61	--   \\
62	--   \\
63	--   \text{Cosine quadrant 0: } & \cos(\theta) = \sin(\frac{\pi}{2} - \theta)
64	--     = \sin( \bar{\theta} )
65	--   \\
66	--   \text{Cosine quadrant 1: } & \cos(\theta + \frac{\pi}{2}) = - \sin(\theta)
67	--   \\
68	--   \text{Cosine quadrant 2: } & \cos(\theta + \pi) = - \sin(\frac{\pi}{2} - \theta)
69	--     = - \sin( \bar{\theta} )
70	--   \\
71	--   \text{Cosine quadrant 3: } & \cos(\theta + \frac{3 \pi}{2}) = \sin(\theta)
72	--
73	-- This shows how both sine and cosine for all four quadrant can be calculated using only sine
74	-- values from the first quadrant (:math:`[0, \pi/2]`).
75	-- In the calculations above we have utilized the fact that a phase of :math:`\pi/2 - \theta`,
76	-- meaning phase in reverse order, is the same as bit-inversion of the phase.
77	--
78	--
79	-- Fixed-point representation
80	-- __________________________
81	--
82	-- When we want to distribute :math:`2^\text{memory_address_width}` number of points over the phase
83	-- range :math:`[0, \pi / 2[`, we use
84	--
85	-- .. math::
86	--
87	--   \text{phase_increment} \equiv \frac{\pi / 2}{2^\text{memory_address_width}}.
88	--
89	-- To achieve the symmetry we aim for in the discussion above, we offset the phase by half an LSB:
90	--
91	-- .. math::
92	--
93	--   \phi \equiv \frac{\text{phase_increment}}{2}.
94	--
95	-- This gives a total phase of
96	--
97	-- .. math::
98	--
99	--   \theta(i) \equiv i \times \text{phase_increment} + \phi.
100	--
101	-- We also have an amplitude-quantization given by the ``memory_data_width`` generic.
102	-- This gives a maximum amplitude of
103	--
104	-- .. math::
105	--
106	--   A \equiv 2^\text{memory_data_width} - 1.
107	--
108	-- With this established, we can calculate the memory values as
109	--
110	-- .. math::
111	--
112	--   \text{mem} (i) = \text{int} \left(  A \times \sin(\theta(i)) \right),
113	--     \forall i \in [0, 2^\text{memory_address_width} - 1].
114	--
115	-- As can be seen in the trigonometric identities above, the resulting output sine value from this
116	-- entity is negated in some some quadrants.
117	-- This gives an output range of :math:`[-A, A]`.
118	-- Where fixed-point :math:`A` is equivalent to floating-point :math:`1.0`.
119	--
120	--
121	-- Performance
122	-- ___________
123	--
124	-- Samples in memory are stored with ``memory_data_width`` bits, and the quadrant handling discussed
125	-- above adds one more sign bit.
126	-- The only source of noise and distortion is the digital quantization noise when storing sine
127	-- values with a fixed-point representation in memory.
128	--
129	-- Hence the result from this entity has SNDR and SFDR equal to ``memory_data_width + 1`` ENOB.
130	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	
132	library ieee;
133	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
134	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
135	use ieee.math_real.all;
136	
137	library common;
138	use common.types_pkg.all;
139	
140	
141	entity sine_lookup is
142	  generic (
143	    -- The data width of sinusoid samples in ROM.
144	    -- Affects the SNDR/SFDR of the result.
145	    memory_data_width : positive;
146	    -- The number of bits in the address of sine samples in ROM.
147	    -- ROM will hold '2 ** memory_address_width' samples.
148	    -- Affects the frequency resolution.
149	    memory_address_width : positive;
150	    -- Enable the output signals you want to calculate.
151	    enable_sine : boolean := false;
152	    enable_cosine : boolean := false;
153	    enable_minus_sine : boolean := false;
154	    enable_minus_cosine : boolean := false
155	  );
156	  port(
157	    clk : in std_ulogic;
158	    --# {{}}
159	    input_valid : in std_ulogic;
160	    input_phase : in u_unsigned(memory_address_width + 2 - 1 downto 0);
161	    --# {{}}
162	    result_valid : out std_ulogic := '0';
163	    result_sine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
164	    result_cosine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
165	    result_minus_sine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
166	    result_minus_cosine : out u_signed(memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0')
167	  );
168	
169	  -- 'result' is valid this many clock cycles after 'input' is valid.
170	  attribute latency : positive;
171	  attribute latency of sine_lookup : entity is 3;
172	end entity;
173	
174	
175	architecture a of sine_lookup is
176	
177	  constant our_latency : positive := sine_lookup'latency;
178	
179	  constant any_sine_enabled : boolean := enable_sine or enable_minus_sine;
180	  constant any_cosine_enabled : boolean := enable_cosine or enable_minus_cosine;
181	
182	  constant memory_depth : positive := 2 ** memory_address_width;
183	
184	  subtype memory_address_t is natural range 0 to memory_depth - 1;
185	  signal sine_address, cosine_address : memory_address_t := 0;
186	
187	  signal memory_sine, memory_cosine : u_unsigned(memory_data_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
188	
189	begin
190	
191	  assert any_sine_enabled or any_cosine_enabled
192	    report "Enable at least one signal or this entity is quite useless"
193	    severity failure;
194	
195	
196	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
197	  memory_block : block
198	    type sine_table_t is array (memory_address_t) of u_unsigned(memory_data_width - 1 downto 0);
199	
200	    function calculate_sine_quadrant_table return sine_table_t is
201	      -- We aim to fill the memory with sine samples for a phase range of [0, pi/2[.
202	      constant index_phase_increment : real := MATH_PI_OVER_2 / real(memory_depth);
203	      -- Offset so that quadrant extrapolation works (see module documentation).
204	      constant phase_offset : real := index_phase_increment / 2.0;
205	
206	      -- Amplitude is e.g. [0, 511] (including)
207	      -- Note that since we are calculating only the first quadrant, the values are
208	      -- always non-negative.
209	      constant fix_point_scale : real := real(2 ** memory_data_width - 1);
210	
211	      variable phase, value, value_scaled, value_rounded : real := 0.0;
212	      variable result : sine_table_t := (others => (others => '0'));
213	    begin
214	      for idx in result'range loop
215	        phase := real(idx) * index_phase_increment + phase_offset;
216	
217	        value := sin(phase);
218	        value_scaled := value * fix_point_scale;
219	        value_rounded := round(value_scaled);
220	
221	        result(idx) := to_unsigned(integer(value_rounded), result(0)'length);
222	      end loop;
223	
224	      return result;
225	    end function;
226	
227	    constant sine_quadrant_table : sine_table_t := calculate_sine_quadrant_table;
228	
229	    signal memory_sine_m1, memory_cosine_m1 : u_unsigned(memory_data_width - 1 downto 0) := (
230	      others => '0'
231	    );
232	  begin
233	
234	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	    memory : process
236	    begin
237	      wait until rising_edge(clk);
238	
239	      -- Read with the BRAM output register enabled, for better timing on the output side.
240	
241	      if any_sine_enabled then
242	        memory_sine_m1 <= sine_quadrant_table(sine_address);
243	        memory_sine <= memory_sine_m1;
244	      end if;
245	
246	      if any_cosine_enabled then
247	        memory_cosine_m1 <= sine_quadrant_table(cosine_address);
248	        memory_cosine <= memory_cosine_m1;
249	      end if;
250	    end process;
251	
252	  end block;
253	
254	
255	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
256	  quadrant_block : block
257	    signal input_quadrant, result_quadrant : u_unsigned(1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
258	    signal input_quadrant_phase : u_unsigned(memory_address_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
259	
260	    signal memory_sine_signed, memory_cosine_signed : u_signed(
261	      memory_data_width + 1 - 1 downto 0
262	    ) := (others => '0');
263	
264	    -- 'valid' is simple piped along all the way.
265	    signal valid_pipe : std_ulogic_vector(1 to our_latency) := (others => '0');
266	    -- 'quadrant' is used in the pipeline step before the result.
267	    signal quadrant_pipe : unsigned_vec_t(1 to our_latency - 1)(input_quadrant'range) := (
268	      others => (others => '0')
269	    );
270	  begin
271	
272	    input_quadrant <= input_phase(input_phase'high downto input_phase'high - 1);
273	    input_quadrant_phase <= input_phase(input_phase'high - 2 downto 0);
274	
275	
276	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
277	    assign_address : process(all)
278	      variable sine_address_slv, cosine_address_slv : u_unsigned(input_quadrant_phase'range) := (
279	        others => '0'
280	      );
281	    begin
282	      -- Could change this to a clocked process to have the BRAM address be driven by FF instead
283	      -- of LUT.
284	      -- For wide addresses with both sine and cosine the fanout of 'input_quadrant' might
285	      -- be a problem.
286	      -- Making that change increases the FF utilization, but slightly lowers the LUT utilization.
287	
288	      -- Read out in reverse in the appropriate quadrants (see documentation).
289	      if input_quadrant = 1 or input_quadrant = 3 then
290	        sine_address_slv := not input_quadrant_phase;
291	        cosine_address_slv := input_quadrant_phase;
292	      else
293	        sine_address_slv := input_quadrant_phase;
294	        cosine_address_slv := not input_quadrant_phase;
295	      end if;
296	
297	      if any_sine_enabled then
298	        sine_address <= to_integer(sine_address_slv);
299	      end if;
300	
301	      if any_cosine_enabled then
302	        cosine_address <= to_integer(cosine_address_slv);
303	      end if;
304	    end process;
305	
306	
307	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
308	    pipe : process
309	    begin
310	      wait until rising_edge(clk);
311	
312	      -- Pipe the status signals from the input of the memory to the output.
313	      valid_pipe <= input_valid & valid_pipe(valid_pipe'left to valid_pipe'right - 1);
314	      quadrant_pipe <= (
315	        input_quadrant & quadrant_pipe(quadrant_pipe'left to quadrant_pipe'right - 1)
316	      );
317	    end process;
318	
319	    result_valid <= valid_pipe(valid_pipe'right);
320	    result_quadrant <= quadrant_pipe(quadrant_pipe'right);
321	
322	    memory_sine_signed <= '0' & u_signed(memory_sine);
323	    memory_cosine_signed <= '0' & u_signed(memory_cosine);
324	
325	
326	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
327	    assign_result : process
328	      variable sine_in_negative_quadrant, cosine_in_negative_quadrant : boolean := false;
329	    begin
330	      wait until rising_edge(clk);
331	
332	      -- Negate result in the appropriate quadrants (see documentation).
333	      sine_in_negative_quadrant := result_quadrant = 2 or result_quadrant = 3;
334	
335	      if enable_sine then
336	        if sine_in_negative_quadrant then
337	          result_sine <= - memory_sine_signed;
338	        else
339	          result_sine <= memory_sine_signed;
340	        end if;
341	      end if;
342	
343	      if enable_minus_sine then
344	        if sine_in_negative_quadrant then
345	          result_minus_sine <= memory_sine_signed;
346	        else
347	          result_minus_sine <= - memory_sine_signed;
348	        end if;
349	      end if;
350	
351	      cosine_in_negative_quadrant := result_quadrant = 1 or result_quadrant = 2;
352	
353	      if enable_cosine then
354	        if cosine_in_negative_quadrant then
355	          result_cosine <= - memory_cosine_signed;
356	        else
357	          result_cosine <= memory_cosine_signed;
358	        end if;
359	      end if;
360	
361	      if enable_minus_cosine then
362	        if cosine_in_negative_quadrant then
363	          result_minus_cosine <= memory_cosine_signed;
364	        else
365	          result_minus_cosine <= - memory_cosine_signed;
366	        end if;
367	      end if;
368	    end process;
369	
370	  end block;
371	
372	end architecture;
373

1	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	-- Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
3	--
4	-- This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
5	-- peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
6	-- https://hdl-modules.com
7	-- https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
8	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9	-- Core that calculates the first-order Taylor expansion of sinusoid function
10	--
11	-- .. math::
12	--
13	--   f(x) \approx f(a) + e \times f'(a)
14	--
15	-- with fixed-point numbers that fit in a DSP48.
16	-- This core is to be used in :ref:`sine_generator.sine_calculator`, and is not really suitable
17	-- for other purposes.
18	--
19	-- .. warning::
20	--
21	--   This is an internal core.
22	--   The interface might change without notice.
23	-- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	
25	library ieee;
26	use ieee.numeric_std.all;
27	use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
28	
29	library common;
30	use common.attribute_pkg.all;
31	
32	library math;
33	
34	
35	entity taylor_expansion_core is
36	  generic (
37	    sinusoid_width : positive;
38	    error_factor_width : positive;
39	    error_factor_fractional_width : positive;
40	    result_width : positive;
41	    minus_derivative : boolean
42	  );
43	  port(
44	    clk : in std_ulogic;
45	    --# {{}}
46	    input_value : in u_signed(sinusoid_width - 1 downto 0);
47	    input_derivative : in u_signed(sinusoid_width - 1 downto 0);
48	    input_error_factor : in u_signed(error_factor_width - 1 downto 0);
49	    --# {{}}
50	    result_value : out u_signed(result_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0')
51	  );
52	
53	  -- 'result' is valid this many clock cycles after 'input' is valid.
54	  attribute latency : positive;
55	  attribute latency of taylor_expansion_core : entity is 2;
56	end entity;
57	
58	architecture a of taylor_expansion_core is
59	
60	  -- The 'memory_data_width' is very typically 18, yielding a 'derivative' value of 19 bits.
61	  -- In order to map to a DSP48 it would be lovely if it was just 18 bits.
62	  -- We have asserted through simulation that this is not limiting the performance.
63	  constant derivative18_width : positive := minimum(input_derivative'length, 18);
64	  -- Number of bits we trimmed to get to the desired width.
65	  constant derivative18_trim : natural := input_derivative'length - derivative18_width;
66	
67	  -- After all trimming there are this many fractional bits left in the 'derivative' term.
68	  constant derivative_term_fractional_width : natural := (
69	    error_factor_fractional_width - derivative18_trim
70	  );
71	
72	  -- We add two terms, so we need this many guard bits.
73	  constant sum_num_guard_bits : positive := 1;
74	  -- Terms may not be wider than this in order to avoid overflow.
75	  constant max_sum_term_width : positive := 48 - sum_num_guard_bits;
76	  -- Maximum number of steps we can shift up the 'value' term.
77	  constant max_sum_term_fractional_width : positive := max_sum_term_width - input_value'length;
78	
79	  -- Number of fractional bits in the 'derivative' term when we have trimmed it to make the
80	  -- addition fit in a DSP48.
81	  constant derivative_term48_fractional_width : natural := minimum(
82	    derivative_term_fractional_width, max_sum_term_fractional_width
83	  );
84	  -- Number of bits we trimmed to get to the desired width.
85	  -- When operands are small enough, this will be zero.
86	  -- In that case, the 'derivative' term multiplication and the addition with 'value' term
87	  -- can fit in the same DSP48.
88	  constant derivative_term48_trim : natural := (
89	    derivative_term_fractional_width - derivative_term48_fractional_width
90	  );
91	
92	  -- When adding the 'value' and 'derivative' terms to form the result of Taylor expansion,
93	  -- we must pad the 'value' so that the binal points align.
94	  -- Our words are aligned something like this, we don't really know:
95	  --
96	  -- Derivative term            |----------------------|
97	  -- Value term       |------------------10000000000000|
98	  -- Sum             |---------------------------------|
99	  -- Result           |------------------|
100	  --
101	  -- Derivative term                    |--------------|
102	  -- Value term       |------------------10000000000000|
103	  -- Sum             |---------------------------------|
104	  -- Result           |------------------|
105	  --
106	  -- The 'value' term padding has a '1' in the MSB in order to get +0.5 rounding of the result,
107	  -- which yields greater performance.
108	  function get_value_term_padding return u_signed is
109	    variable result : u_signed(derivative_term48_fractional_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
110	  begin
111	    result(result'high) := '1';
112	    return result;
113	  end function;
114	  constant value_term_padding : u_signed(derivative_term48_fractional_width - 1 downto 0) := (
115	    get_value_term_padding
116	  );
117	
118	  signal second_stage_value_term48 : u_signed(
119	    input_value'length + value_term_padding'length - 1 downto 0
120	  ) := (others => '0');
121	  signal second_stage_derivative_term48 : u_signed(
122	    derivative18_width + input_error_factor'length - derivative_term48_trim - 1 downto 0
123	  ) := (others => '0');
124	
125	  attribute use_dsp of second_stage_value_term48 : signal is "yes";
126	  attribute use_dsp of second_stage_derivative_term48 : signal is "yes";
127	
128	begin
129	
130	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	  print : process
132	  begin
133	    report "======================================================================";
134	    report "derivative18_width = " & integer'image(derivative18_width);
135	    report "derivative18_trim = " & integer'image(derivative18_trim);
136	
137	    report "======================================================================";
138	    report "derivative_term_fractional_width = " & integer'image(derivative_term_fractional_width);
139	    report "max_sum_term_fractional_width = " & integer'image(max_sum_term_fractional_width);
140	    report (
141	      "derivative_term48_fractional_width = " & integer'image(derivative_term48_fractional_width)
142	    );
143	    report "derivative_term48_trim = " & integer'image(derivative_term48_trim);
144	
145	    wait;
146	  end process;
147	
148	
149	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
150	  second_stage : block
151	    signal derivative18 : u_signed(derivative18_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
152	
153	    signal derivative_term : u_signed(derivative18_width + error_factor_width - 1 downto 0) := (
154	      others => '0'
155	    );
156	
157	    attribute use_dsp of derivative_term : signal is "yes";
158	  begin
159	
160	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
161	    assert derivative18'length <= 18
162	      report "Will not map nicely to DSP48. Got " & integer'image(derivative18'length)
163	      severity failure;
164	
165	    assert input_error_factor'length <= 25
166	      report (
167	        "Will not map nicely to DSP48. Got "
168	        & integer'image(input_error_factor'length)
169	      )
170	      severity failure;
171	
172	
173	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
174	    calculate_second_stage : process
175	    begin
176	      wait until rising_edge(clk);
177	
178	      derivative_term <= derivative18 * input_error_factor;
179	
180	      -- Hopefully this can use the input register of the DSP48.
181	      second_stage_value_term48 <= input_value & value_term_padding;
182	    end process;
183	
184	    derivative18 <= input_derivative(input_derivative'high downto derivative18_trim);
185	
186	    second_stage_derivative_term48 <= derivative_term(
187	      derivative_term'high downto derivative_term48_trim
188	    );
189	
190	  end block;
191	
192	
193	  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
194	  third_stage : block
195	    -- Assumes that the 'value' term is wider than the 'derivative' term, which is checked below.
196	    -- If 'derivative' term is wider, we must take into account that it contains a redundant sign
197	    -- bit due to unsigned -> signed conversion.
198	    constant sum_width : positive := second_stage_value_term48'length + sum_num_guard_bits;
199	    signal sum : u_signed(sum_width - 1 downto 0) := (others => '0');
200	
201	    -- After addition, some LSBs are removed.
202	    signal sum_result_range : u_signed(result_value'length + sum_num_guard_bits - 1 downto 0) := (
203	      others => '0'
204	    );
205	
206	    attribute use_dsp of sum : signal is "yes";
207	  begin
208	
209	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
210	    assert second_stage_derivative_term48'length >= value_term_padding'length
211	      report "Taylor addition is a null operation"
212	      severity failure;
213	
214	    assert result_value'length = input_value'length
215	      report "Some assumptions do not hold unless this is true"
216	      severity failure;
217	
218	    assert second_stage_value_term48'length > second_stage_derivative_term48'length
219	      report "Some assumptions do not hold unless this is true"
220	      severity failure;
221	
222	    assert second_stage_value_term48'length < 48
223	      report "Will not map nicely to DSP48. Got " & integer'image(second_stage_value_term48'length)
224	      severity failure;
225	
226	    assert second_stage_derivative_term48'length < 48
227	      report (
228	        "Will not map nicely to DSP48. Got " & integer'image(second_stage_derivative_term48'length)
229	      )
230	      severity failure;
231	
232	    assert sum'length <= 48
233	      report "Will not map nicely to DSP48. Got " & integer'image(sum'length)
234	      severity failure;
235	
236	    assert result_value'length < sum'length
237	      report (
238	        "Result is too wide. Got "
239	        & integer'image(result_value'length)
240	        & " "
241	        & integer'image(sum'length)
242	      )
243	      severity failure;
244	
245	
246	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
247	    calculate_third_stage : process
248	    begin
249	      wait until rising_edge(clk);
250	
251	      -- Note that the 'derivative' term contains a redundant sign bit due to
252	      -- unsigned -> signed conversion.
253	      -- If the addition were done in LUTs we would strip that bit, which would save some LUTs.
254	      -- However, if we ever want the multiplication and addition to map to the same DSP48,
255	      -- we can't do that.
256	      -- Due to the way the internals of the DSP is constructed.
257	      -- And we would also not save anything by doing that.
258	      if minus_derivative then
259	        sum <= resize(second_stage_value_term48, sum'length) - second_stage_derivative_term48;
260	      else
261	        sum <= resize(second_stage_value_term48, sum'length) + second_stage_derivative_term48;
262	      end if;
263	    end process;
264	
265	    -- Strip LSBs.
266	    sum_result_range <= sum(sum'high downto sum'length - sum_result_range'length);
267	
268	
269	    ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
270	    -- The sum overflows at almost every peak in every configuration.
271	    -- We have to saturate the result so that the full range, or at least some well-defined range,
272	    -- of the output word is used.
273	    -- The overflow is so small that the SFDR of the result is still good enough in all
274	    -- simulations even with saturation.
275	    saturate_signed_inst : entity math.saturate_signed
276	      generic map(
277	        input_width => sum_result_range'length,
278	        result_width => result_value'length
279	      )
280	      port map(
281	        clk => clk,
282	        --
283	        input_value => sum_result_range,
284	        result_value => result_value
285	      );
286	
287	  end block;
288	
289	end architecture;
290

1	This module contains a flexible and robust sinusoidal waveform generator written in VHDL.
2	Also known as a direct digital synthesizer (DDS), numerically controlled oscillator (NCO), or
3	a sine/sinus wave generator.
4	It supports an SFDR of 192 dB in fractional phase mode, and theoretically unlimited SFDR in
5	integer phase mode.
6	Both sine and cosine outputs are supported, for I/Q modulation and other applications.
7	
8	See the website for an overview and documentation of what is available:
9	https://hdl-modules.com/modules/sine_generator/sine_generator.html
10

file.content:
This module contains a flexible and robust sinusoidal waveform generator written in VHDL.
Also known as a direct digital synthesizer (DDS), numerically controlled oscillator (NCO), or
a sine/sinus wave generator.
It supports an SFDR of 192 dB in fractional phase mode, and theoretically unlimited SFDR in
integer phase mode.
Both sine and cosine outputs are supported, for I/Q modulation and other applications.

See the website for an overview and documentation of what is available:
https://hdl-modules.com/modules/sine_generator/sine_generator.html

1	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
2	# Copyright (c) Lukas Vik. All rights reserved.
3	#
4	# This file is part of the hdl-modules project, a collection of reusable, high-quality,
5	# peer-reviewed VHDL building blocks.
6	# https://hdl-modules.com
7	# https://github.com/hdl-modules/hdl-modules
8	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
14	
15	from tsfpga.examples.vivado.project import TsfpgaExampleVivadoNetlistProject
16	from tsfpga.module import BaseModule
17	from tsfpga.vivado.build_result_checker import (
18	    DspBlocks,
19	    EqualTo,
20	    Ffs,
21	    MaximumLogicLevel,
22	    Ramb18,
23	    Ramb36,
24	    TotalLuts,
25	)
26	
27	if TYPE_CHECKING:
28	    from collections.abc import Callable
29	
30	    from matplotlib.axes import Axes
31	    from numpy import ndarray
32	    from vunit.ui import VUnit
33	
34	# Import locally, for performance in the typical use case.
35	# Especially important here since we import some huge packages (numpy, scipy, etc).
36	# ruff: noqa: PLC0415
37	
38	
39	class Module(BaseModule):
40	    def setup_vunit(
41	        self,
42	        vunit_proj: VUnit,
43	        inspect: bool = False,
44	        **kwargs: Any,  # noqa: ANN401, ARG002
45	    ) -> None:
46	        self._setup_lookup_tests(vunit_proj=vunit_proj, inspect=inspect)
47	        self._setup_generator_tests(vunit_proj=vunit_proj, inspect=inspect)

49	    def _setup_lookup_tests(self, vunit_proj: VUnit, inspect: bool) -> None:
50	        def get_post_check(generics: dict[str, Any]) -> Callable:
51	            def post_check(output_path: str) -> bool:
52	                return self.lookup_post_check(
53	                    output_path=Path(output_path), generics=generics, inspect=inspect
54	                )
55	
56	            return post_check
57	
58	        tb = vunit_proj.library(self.library_name).test_bench("tb_sine_lookup")
59	        for memory_address_width in [7, 12]:
60	            for memory_data_width in [8, 18]:
61	                generics = {
62	                    "memory_address_width": memory_address_width,
63	                    "memory_data_width": memory_data_width,
64	                }
65	                self.add_vunit_config(
66	                    test=tb, generics=generics, post_check=get_post_check(generics=generics)
67	                )
